summary: Family scam protection across calls, texts, voicemail, and Scamwise checks.
editor_note: Consumer security feels different when the threat is a fake grandchild call, not a malware pop-up. Savi bundles voicemail screening, text filtering, live-call monitoring, and Scamwise checks into one family plan. Unlike antivirus for devices, the bet is protection for moments when people are confused, pressured, and mid-conversation. The risk is whether phone-level permissions and model accuracy are enough to earn daily trust.

product_evidence.claim: The official site presents Savi as an iOS and Android family protection app covering suspicious calls, texts, voicemail, and Scamwise checks.
The official download page and Scamwise checker provide the app entry point and a public scam-validation surface linked from Savi's homepage.
The funding report says Savi launched with a $7M Seed led by Acrew Capital and cites more than 100,000 Scamwise submissions before launch.

market_context.primary_user: Families trying to protect parents, grandparents, and household members from phone, text, and AI-voice scams.
market_context.differentiation: Savi protects the moment of communication itself through call, text, voicemail, and family alert surfaces rather than only scanning devices or emails.
market_context.why_now: AI voice cloning and impersonation scams are making family-level phone protection more urgent, while mobile apps can now mediate calls, voicemails, and texts.

risks.claim: The falsifiable risk is whether users keep the app installed and permissioned if live-call monitoring produces false positives or misses urgent scam attempts.
risks.basis: VentureDex assessment from the official product page and seed funding report.
